user_query:
I want to level up from "can script charts" to "competent at data storytelling with Python for business dashboards" in 6 weeks while working full-time. Please read my context and constraints from the files under input/ and build a complete, evidence-based learning system. Deliverables and rules:

Read first:
- input/goals.yaml
- input/background.json
- input/resources_candidates.csv
- input/calendar_constraints.txt

Then produce these files under output/ with the exact filenames and structures:
1) output/learning_brief.yaml
- Use the learning project brief structure (target skill, why, target_level, success_looks_like, deadline, hours_per_week, total_estimated_hours, current_level, related_skills, blockers, accountability). Incorporate any relevant details from input/ files. Be specific and observable.

2) output/sub_skill_map.yaml
- 10–20 sub-skills for "data storytelling with Python for business dashboards".
- For each sub-skill: name, importance (1–5), frequency (1–5), score (importance×frequency), depends_on (list), status.
- Include a top-level critical_path listing the top 3–5 sub-skills chosen by 80/20 analysis, ordered.

3) output/resources.csv
- Apply the 3-Source Rule: exactly 3 rows, one of each type: primary, alternative, practice.
- Columns (exact names): type,title,author,year,practice_ratio_pct,credibility_score,recency_score,practice_ratio_score,progression_score,community_score,weighted_total
- Score each factor 0–10, then compute weighted_total as: credibility*2 + recency*1.5 + practice_ratio_score*2 + progression*1.5 + community_score*1. Make sure weighted_total ≤ 80 and matches that formula (round to one decimal). Choose items from input/resources_candidates.csv and justify the type selection implicitly via titles and practice_ratio_pct.

4) output/learning_protocol.md
- Design three 45–60 minute sessions using the ARPD cycle and Feynman Technique, explicitly labeled Session 1, Session 2, Session 3. Each session must contain the headings ABSORB, RETRIEVE, PRACTICE, DEBRIEF and show interleaving across different sub-skills.

5) output/spaced_repetition.json
- Include a spacing_schedule array with objects for review_number 1–6 and after_days exactly [1,3,7,14,30,90].
- Include a flashcard_design_rules array with at least 6 rules that follow best practice (one fact per card, hard direction, cloze, context, example, delete easy cards).

6) output/project_plan.yaml
- Use a 4-week learning project framework oriented to a minimum viable dashboard deliverable that solves a real business problem from the inputs. Include: name, target_skill, secondary_skills, scope (minimum viable), stretch_goals (list), deadline, public (true/false), milestones with week_1, week_2, week_3, week_4.
- Add a short note enforcing the 30% Rule (why scope is ~30% new).

7) output/deliberate_practice.md
- One deliberate practice session focused on a precise weakness you infer from the inputs. Use the headings IDENTIFY, DESIGN, EXECUTE, GET FEEDBACK, ADJUST.

8) output/knowledge_notes.jsonl
- At least 3 Zettelkasten-lite concept notes, one per line as JSON. Fields: id, title, source, explanation, example, connections (array with at least 2 items), application, questions.

9) output/weekly_review_template.yaml
- Provide a weekly_review template with all fields: week_of, hours_logged, sessions_completed, sub_skills_progressed (list with objects name/from_level/to_level/evidence), biggest_win, biggest_struggle, next_week_focus, difficulty_rating, enjoyment_rating, confidence_delta.

10) output/quality_score.yaml
- Score this plan using a 100-point rubric with these weighted dimensions: clarity (weight 2), decomposition (1.5), active_methods (2), spaced_practice (1.5), feedback_loop (1.5), progress_tracking (1), sustainability (1.5). For each: include score (0–10) and brief rationale. Include weighted_total (out of 115) and normalized_total (0–100) computed as weighted_total/115*100, rounded to one decimal.

Important:
- Use only the information in input/ to tailor the plan. If something is missing, state your assumption briefly in the relevant file rather than blocking.
- Keep all files text-based and ensure the specified structures and field names are exact so they can be programmatically checked.
- In resources.csv, ensure the three types are present exactly once each and the weighted_total equals the formula.
- Reflect the availability in input/calendar_constraints.txt when proposing hours_per_week and milestones.

I will judge quality on clarity, prioritization (80/20), specificity, and realism. Please proceed.
